1.title: Doup!
1.text: I got off the plane three hours late. My initial plan was to walk to my destination. It would take around one hour depending on my internal compass. But as I was late already and it was 9 pm. I decided to take the bus. At the bus stop a black guy is shouting all sorts of weird stuff, so as he sees me he shouts: "What's up man!?" I decided to what's up him back, but my native tongue took over and shouted "Doup"! It worked. He stopped shouting and started looking confused. I asked him about the bus, but he was to stunned to answer. I decided to walk away.

2.title: American Dream
2.text: "When I came to America I had nothing. I love my job. I come here everyday because I love my job. Customers can see that and so they come back". I had just turned on my laptop trying to figure out the WiFI access code, when I really heard The American Dream in motion. The owner of the coffee shop was constructively telling his niece how to act towards customers and how to put your soul into every cup of copy that is handed over the counter.

3.title: Beat It
3.text: So apparently San Francisco is made of hills. Steep hills. Back in my country we would probably call them mountains. And when you pass the peak, look! There is another one! Mount Doom. Tourists gladly rent bicycles without knowing they are going to face the toughest challenges of their lives. Men in their late forties are trying to catch up to their wives everywhere. The asian bicycle wave is also pretty much at large here. Today in the afternoon when I was heading home there was this little asian guy with a bike getting ready. The bike itself is fitted with two larges bags and a small vertical flagpole with a flag on top. It sort of looks like the wooden stick parents use to hold on to their kids when they teach them to bike. On the back of the bike is mounted a small ghettoblaster, for no apparent reason. This asian guy is ready. He gets on the bike, turns on Michael Jackson's Beat It on the ghettoblaster and full throttles up the hill. I couldn't stop myself laughing out loud! Lol.

4.title: Not bad huh?
4.text: I agreed with an american that The Golden Gate Bridge is truly an astonishing sight at 7:30 in the morning.

5.title: My spirit friend
5.text: The first bus I got on malfunctioned when i put 5 dollars into the machine. The bus driver looked at me like I was an idiot and handed me over two tickets. Doh.

6.title: Tall but crumbling
6.text: Jetlagged as I was I woke op really early. I decided to go to the Financial District to see where the money is. As I get out of the bus/train a homeless man is eating spaghetti directly from a dumbster shadowed by some of the tallest buildings I have seen in my life. It just feels weird witnessing people in such bad shape when you are not used to it. And after a while it feels weird being okay with being used to it...

7.title: Go big or go home
7.text: I have been here two days and I have already managed to buy one way too large cup of iced coffee, order three times a many weird fries as I could possibly eat and totally undercook the largest organic eggs I have ever seen. Four minutes in boiling water is clearly not enough.

8.title: Different worlds
8.text: Try walking down Valencia Street. Grab a coffee. Turn around and walk back on Mission Street. Two different worlds.

9.title: Public bus
9.text: It was about time I headed home from Mission District. Bus 22. I got on the bus with some other being. I do not know wether it was a man or a woman. He/she was so hunched over, dressed in fully winters clothes even though it was summer and had a walking assistance bar. I do not know how he/she was even able to stay alive. Next stop he/she goes off the bus with help again from the bus' lift. Now the lift is used to get a disabled man aboard. He is in a wheelchair without both front wheels so it can only go backwards. I almost puked at the smell of him. He smelled worse than the worst pissoir I have ever been near. What a depressing situation.

10.title: Alsian invasion
10.text: This morning I was on a bus with an asian driver and 25 passengers. All from Asia. An asian man actually talked to me. In asian! What did he expect?

11.title: I was like...
11.text: It is actually true that some people start all of their sentences with "I was like" followed quickly by a barrage of words I do not understand.

12.title: It's just a cat
12.text:
I was about to ride downhill mountainbiking with two of the locals in the area. These were their instructions:
"If a mountain lion gets on to one of us, the other two should fight and try to kill it with everything they got. Let's go!"
